project_privacy_data_flows.data_type: Event Data (event name, schema version, app install ID, app session ID, timestamp, user language, anonymous/temp status, domain, event-specific payload fields)
project_privacy_data_flows.description: Event Platform Client (EPC) collects and submits analytics events to the Wikimedia event intake service. Events are batched in an input buffer and periodically bursted via HTTP POST.

flow_steps.code: public func submit<E: EventInterface>(stream: Stream, event: E, domain: String? = nil, needsMinimal: Bool = false, completion: (() -> Void)? = nil) { let date = Date(); encodeQueue.async { self._submit(stream: stream, event: event, date: date, domain: domain, needsMinimal: needsMinimal); completion?() } }
flow_steps.details: Various app components call EventPlatformClient.submit() to log events (e.g., LoginFunnel, DonateFunnel, NavigationEventsFunnel, SessionsFunnel, etc.). Each event is tagged with a stream name and schema version.

flow_steps.code: private func _submit<E: EventInterface>(...) { let meta = Meta(stream: stream, id: UUID(), domain: domain); let eventPayload = EventBody<E>(meta: meta, appInstallID: appInstallID, appSessionID: sessionID, dt: date, event: event, isAnon: isAnon, isTemp: isTemp, primaryLanguage: primaryLanguage); let data = try encoder.encode(eventPayload) }
flow_steps.details: Each event is wrapped with metadata including: appInstallID (from cross-process UserDefaults), sessionID, timestamp, anonymous status, temporary account status, and primary language.

flow_steps.code: Events are appended to an input buffer array before being bursted to the remote endpoint
flow_steps.details: Events are first queued in an in-memory input buffer, then periodically flushed to the server via HTTP POST.

flow_steps.code: Periodic HTTP POST to remote intake endpoint with batched events
flow_steps.details: Buffered events are sent in batches to the Wikimedia event intake service endpoint via HTTPS POST.

project_privacy_data_flows.data_type: Login Funnel Events (action: impression/login_start/logout/login_success/createaccount_start/createaccount_success, category, label, timestamp)
project_privacy_data_flows.description: Login funnel tracks user progression through the login and account creation flow for analytics.

flow_steps.code: private func logEvent(category: EventCategoryMEP, label: EventLabelMEP?, action: Action, measure: Double? = nil) { let event = LoginFunnel.Event(measure_time: measureTime, action: action, label: label, category: category); EventPlatformClient.shared.submit(stream: .login, event: event) }
flow_steps.details: Login-related user actions (impression, login start, success, logout, create account) are logged as analytics events via the EventPlatformClient.

flow_steps.code: EventPlatformClient._submit packages the login event with appInstallID, sessionID, timestamp, and metadata before transmission
flow_steps.details: The login funnel event is packaged with standard metadata (appInstallID, sessionID, dt, isAnon, primaryLanguage) and encoded as JSON.

flow_steps.code: Batched login events are sent via HTTP POST to the Wikimedia event intake service endpoint
flow_steps.details: Login funnel events are batched with other analytics events and periodically sent to the remote intake service via HTTPS.

project_privacy_data_flows.data_type: Session Data (session ID, session length, timestamp, user interactions, scroll depth, page views, navigation events)
project_privacy_data_flows.description: Session tracking via SessionsFunnel and NavigationEventsFunnel monitors user navigation patterns and session activity.

flow_steps.code: Various session events logged: session_start, session_end, navigation events, scroll events
flow_steps.details: App session lifecycle events (start, end) and user navigation events (page view, scroll, tab switch) are tracked throughout the app session.

flow_steps.code: Session events packaged with appInstallID, sessionID, dt, and event-specific payload fields
flow_steps.details: Session and navigation events are assembled with metadata and queued for transmission.

flow_steps.code: Events flushed from buffer to remote endpoint
flow_steps.details: Session events are batched and sent to the event intake endpoint via HTTPS POST.
